summary: Ukraine received an additional IRIS‑T air‑defence launcher as Russia launched over 2,300 drones, 1,560 guided bombs and 108 missiles this week. Russian forces struck the Saratov oil refinery and Ukrainian infrastructure in multiple oblasts with UAVs, while the Ukrainian Third Army Corps claimed control of logistics routes deep in occupied Luhansk. Threats of Iskander‑M ballistic missiles from Bryansk and continued UAV swarms over the north, south and Black Sea corridors keep the front highly active.
key_points: Ukraine received another IRIS‑T air‑defence launcher; Russia launched over 2,300 drones, 1,560 guided bombs and 108 missiles this week (Source: @UkraineNow/81568).
Russian UAVs attacked a post office depot in Dnipro, destroying the building; no casualties reported (Source: @UkraineNow/81570).
Ukrainian Third Army Corps reported control of logistics routes up to 205 km inside occupied Luhansk, striking armor and ammunition depots (Source: @UkraineNow/81575).
Saratov oil refinery suffered multiple UAV strikes, heavily damaging the primary crude‑oil processing unit ELOU‑AVT‑6 (Source: @UkraineNow/81561).
Multiple UAV groups were observed over the Black Sea, Chernihiv, Poltava, Kherson and other regions, indicating broad Russian aerial activity (Source: @kpszsu/63437).
High threat alert issued for possible Iskander‑M ballistic missile launches from Bryansk region (Source: @mon1tor_ua/67313).
UAV attack on Genichesk resulted in one child fatality and six injuries; total casualties later rose to eleven (Source: @tass_agency/378339, @tass_agency/378343).
Ukrainian forces reported two Shahed drones over Rivne area and one over Sarney (Source: @mon1tor_ua/67315).

sections.executive_summary: Ukraine bolstered its air defence with an IRIS‑T launcher as Russia intensified aerial attacks, deploying thousands of drones, guided bombs and missiles. Key Ukrainian units, notably the Third Army Corps, seized logistical corridors deep within occupied Luhansk, striking enemy supplies. Simultaneously, Russian UAV and missile threats, including possible Iskander‑M launches, continue to pressure multiple fronts.

points: The Ukrainian Ministry of Defence confirmed receipt of an additional IRIS‑T surface‑to‑air missile system, enhancing layered air defence against the high volume of Russian UAVs and missiles (Source: @UkraineNow/81568).

Ukrainian forces reported a successful strike on a Russian oil refinery in Saratov, damaging the primary crude‑oil processing unit ELOU‑AVT‑6 and causing fires across several plant sections (Source: @UkraineNow/81561).

The Third Army Corps announced control over logistics routes in Luhansk, reaching 205 km into occupied territory and destroying enemy armored vehicles and ammunition depots (Source: @UkraineNow/81575).
Ukrainian air‑defence units engaged multiple Shahed drones over Rivne and Sarney, preventing further damage to civilian infrastructure (Source: @mon1tor_ua/67315).
A Ukrainian post office depot in Dnipro was hit by a hostile drone, the building was completely destroyed but staff escaped unharmed (Source: @UkraineNow/81570).
Russian forces launched more than 2,300 UAVs, nearly 1,560 guided aviation bombs and 108 rockets in a single week, indicating a sustained aerial offensive (Source: @UkraineNow/81568).
A high‑alert warning was issued for possible Iskander‑M ballistic missile launches from the Bryansk region, reflecting Russian preparation for long‑range strikes (Source: @mon1tor_ua/67313).
UAV groups were observed moving from the Black Sea towards Odessa and from Zhytomyr towards Rivne, suggesting coordinated strike corridors across the north‑south axis (Source: @kpszsu/63437).

Russian UAV attacks on the town of Genichesk caused civilian casualties, including one child fatality and multiple injuries, highlighting the use of drones against populated areas (Source: @tass_agency/378339).
Additional UAV activity was reported on the northern border of Chernihiv near Belarus, indicating potential reconnaissance or strike missions along the frontier (Source: @kpszsu/63428).
UAVs were sighted on the border of Sumy and Kharkiv regions heading towards Poltava, indicating possible eastward strike vectors (Source: @kpszsu/63407).
Multiple drone groups operated over Sumy, Kharkiv and Chernihiv oblasts, with some heading westward towards Poltava, suggesting pressure on supply lines (Source: @kpszsu/63437).
A Ukrainian report noted UAVs near Dnipro's southern‑eastern outskirts with variable courses, potentially targeting industrial zones (Source: @kpszsu/63418).
Russian UAV attacks on the Donetsk area were mentioned in a launch report for KAB missiles, implying combined missile‑drone operations (Source: @kpszsu/63383).
Ukrainian forces reported UAV activity over the Kharkiv city of Barvinkove moving north‑north‑east, possibly surveilling frontline positions (Source: @kpszsu/63378).
UAV groups were observed on the north‑west of Chernihiv along the Belarus border moving westward, indicating potential cross‑border reconnaissance (Source: @kpszsu/63428).
Drone activity was reported on the north of Chernihiv region, adding to the pressure on northern supply routes (Source: @kpszsu/63437).
UAVs were sighted near the Sumy‑Khrivka border area, suggesting Russian aerial presence near the northern front (Source: @eRadarrua/94526).
A high‑threat alert for Iskander‑M missiles from Bryansk underscores the risk of long‑range strikes on northern Ukrainian targets (Source: @mon1tor_ua/67313).
Russian UAV attack on Genichesk, though in the south, was reported alongside increased northern UAV activity, reflecting a multi‑axis approach (Source: @tass_agency/378339).
UAV groups were reported moving from the Black Sea towards Odessa and Mykolaiv, indicating southern maritime strike routes (Source: @kpszsu/63437).
A UAV from Tomakivka was observed heading towards Dnipro, showing southern‑central penetration (Source: @eRadarrua/94538).
UAV activity over the southern Kherson region was noted with a north‑north‑east course, potentially targeting infrastructure (Source: @kpszsu/63402).
Russian drones attacked a facility in Chernihiv region causing a fire that destroyed seven cargo trucks, showing southern‑northern strike capability (Source: @UkraineNow/81566).
UAVs were reported over the southern Odesa port area, suggesting threats to maritime logistics (Source: @eRadarrua/94549).
Russia launched over 2,300 UAVs, 1,560 guided bombs and 108 rockets in a single week, reflecting a high‑tempo aerial campaign (Source: @UkraineNow/81568).
Ukrainian air‑defence intercepted Shahed drones over Rivne and Sarney, preventing further damage (Source: @mon1tor_ua/67315).
UAV attacks on the Saratov refinery caused extensive fires and damage to the primary processing unit (Source: @UkraineNow/81561).
UAV swarms were observed over multiple Ukrainian oblasts, including Sumy, Kharkiv, Chernihiv, Poltava and the Black Sea corridor, indicating widespread aerial pressure (Source: @kpszsu/63437).
A high‑alert for Iskander‑M ballistic missile launches from Bryansk adds a strategic missile dimension to the air threat (Source: @mon1tor_ua/67313).

key_developments: Ukraine received an additional IRIS‑T air‑defence launcher as Russia launched over 2,300 drones, 1,560 guided bombs and 108 missiles this week (Source: @UkraineNow/81568).
Russian UAVs struck the Saratov oil refinery, heavily damaging the ELOU‑AVT‑6 crude‑oil processing unit (Source: @UkraineNow/81561).
The Ukrainian Third Army Corps claimed control of logistics routes up to 205 km inside occupied Luhansk, striking enemy armor and ammunition depots (Source: @UkraineNow/81575).
Multiple UAV groups were observed over the Black Sea, Chernihiv, Poltava, Kherson and other regions, indicating broad Russian aerial activity (Source: @kpszsu/63437).
A high‑threat alert was issued for possible Iskander‑M ballistic missile launches from the Bryansk region (Source: @mon1tor_ua/67313).
UAV attack on Genichesk resulted in one child death and eleven total injuries, showing civilian impact of drone strikes (Source: @tass_agency/378339, @tass_agency/378343).
Ukrainian forces reported two Shahed drones over Rivne and one over Sarney, successfully engaging the threats (Source: @mon1tor_ua/67315).
sections.threat_assessment: Russian aerial pressure remains intense, with large‑scale UAV and missile launches targeting both strategic infrastructure and civilian areas across multiple fronts. The emergence of Iskander‑M ballistic missile threats from Bryansk adds a long‑range strike capability that could challenge Ukrainian air‑defence depth. Ukrainian counter‑measures, including new IRIS‑T systems and successful UAV interceptions, mitigate some risk but the high volume of attacks sustains an elevated threat environment.
sections.regional_response: Western partners continue to supply advanced air‑defence assets, exemplified by the IRIS‑T delivery, while diplomatic channels address cross‑border incidents such as the Romanian drone strike. Regional actors monitor the expanding UAV corridors, especially over the Black Sea and northern borders, to adjust air‑space monitoring and civil defence protocols.
sections.intelligence_notes: Open‑source UAV sightings reveal coordinated swarm patterns moving from the Black Sea towards inland targets, suggesting a layered strike doctrine. The reported damage to the Saratov refinery indicates a strategic focus on Russian energy assets, potentially aiming to disrupt fuel supplies. Continued Ukrainian claims of logistical route control in Luhansk may signal a shift toward offensive deep‑strike operations behind enemy lines.
